titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Spanish Programs for American Western Films
Spanish Programs for American Western Films
titlestmt.author: Sarah Lerner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday December 9, 2019 at 05:52

did.unittitle: Spanish programs for American Western films

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: 173 handbills designed, printed, and distributed in Spain, for American Western films, circa 1931-1973.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Marc Selvaggio, Bookseller on the William Robertson Coe Fund No. 3, 2016.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title of film.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Spanish Programs for Western American Films. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 173 handbills designed, printed, and distributed in Spain, for American Western films, circa 1931-1973. 77 handbills have printed promotional text on verso, describing the film, screening dates and times, movie theater, and other films on the bill.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Spanish Programs for American Western Films is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: La batalla de las colinas del whisky
Duelo a muerte en O.K. corral
El hombre que mató a Billy el Niño
Un minuto para rezar, un segundo para morir
Reza por tu alma... y muere
El sobrino de don Buffalo Bill
El último tren de Gun Hill
Y después le llamaron El Magnífico
